data.title: Crane to sell Engineered Materials unit in $227M deal
data.description:
Crane (NYSE:CR) said post-market Monday it agreed to sell its Engineered Materials business to KPS Capital Partners for $227M, as it takes further steps to focus resources on its two strategic growth platforms: Aerospace & Electronics and Process Flow Technologies.

KPS Special Situations Funds is a family of investment funds wit $21.4B of assets under management.

Crane (CR) said it updated its most recent guidance for FY 2024 adjusted earnings per share of $5.05-$5.20 to reflect the divestment of Engineered Materials, which lowers adjusted EPS to $4.71-$4.86; for Q4, the company expects adjusted EPS from continuing operations of $1.10-$1.25.

data.title: OPEC+ seen prolonging oil output cuts through Q1 at Thursday's meeting - report
data.description:
Crude oil futures gave up early gains to finish flat Monday, as hopes for stronger demand prompted by higher factory activity in China was offset by concerns that the U.S. Federal Reserve will not cut interest rates again at its December meeting.

China's factory activity expanded in November at the fastest pace in five months, but President-elect Trump threatened BRICS nations with 100% tariffs if the nine-nation bloc backed alternatives to the dollar, adding to fears that U.S. interest rates could remain higher for longer.

Meanwhile, doubts are rising about the staying power of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire amid mutual accusations of violations between the two sides in Lebanon.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for January delivery ended +0.1% to $68.10/bbl, snapping a four-session losing streak, and front-month February Brent crude (CO1:COM) closed nearly flat, down a penny at $71.83/bbl.

U.S. natural gas dropped on warmer temperature forecasts after this week, sending the Nymex January front-month contract (NG1:COM) settling -4.4% at $3.213/MMBtu.

ETFs: (XLE), (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Oil and gas related stocks ranked among the day's biggest losers on the S&P 500, including Targa Resources (TRGP) -4.8%, Williams Cos. (WMB) -3.5%, Kinder Morgan (KMI) -2.9% and ONEOK (OKE) -2.8%.

At its delayed meeting on Thursday, OPEC+ likely will extend its latest round of oil production cut until the end of Q1 to provide further support for the weak oil market, several sources told Reuters.

OPEC+ members are holding back 5.86M bbl/day of output, or ~5.7% of global demand, in a series of steps agreed since 2022 to support the market, and a small increase of 180K bbl/day was due to start in January from the eight members involved in the group's most recent cuts of 2.2M bbl/day.

Despite widespread expectations for some sort of extension to production cuts, "in my opinion, the market is sleepwalking into a possible OPEC production increase," Mizuho's Robert Yawger said, noting the United Arab Emirates wants to raise output after investing in capacity, Russia exports less crude but makes more product, and Kazakhstan is working on the Tangiz field to increase production.

The analyst said he doubts OPEC+ would postpone the meeting by five days if "they all agreed they were going to kick the can down the street."

data.title: Delaware judge again rejects Musk's multi-billion dollar pay package
data.description:
A Delaware judge has again rejected Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk’s multi-billion dollar pay package for a second time, leaving the company uncertain as to how to adequately compensate their CEO for a 1,500% share price gain over the last 5 years. Musk’s attorneys attempted to reverse McCormick’s original decision after shareholders approved the figure this past June.

Judge Kathaleen McCormick declined to restore Musk’s $56B compensation and ordered the company to pay $345M in fees to the plaintiffs’ attorneys. In her decision, McCormick said the approval process lacked transparency, and that the Tesla (TSLA) board was “beholden” to Musk.

“Even if a stockholder vote could have a ratifying effect, it could not do so here,” McCormick said in her decision.

Nevertheless, the board argued that any new package would be more expensive as it would include 303M Tesla stock options awarded at a much higher price.

Tesla (TSLA) shares dipped 1.3% in after-hours trading following the news.

data.title: Zscaler CFO Canessa retires after holding position for nearly eight years
data.description:
• Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS) Chief Financial Officer Remo Canessa is retiring after serving in the position for nearly eight years.
  • "His decision to retire is not the result, in whole or in part, of any disagreement with the Company on any matters relating to the Company’s operations, policies or practices," Zscaler clarified in a Form 8-K filing. "The Company has commenced a search for a new Chief Financial Officer, and Mr. Canessa will remain with the Company through the search and transition period pursuant to terms currently under negotiation."
  • Prior to his position at Zscaler, Canessa served as a CFO at Infoblox for 11 years and as CFO at NetScreen for three.

data.title: Gold tumbles as Trump's newest tariff threat boosts dollar
data.description:
Gold futures fell Monday to start both the new week and month, pressured by a stronger dollar after President-elect Trump warned BRICS nations that he would require commitments that they would not move to create a new currency.

Trump threatened 100% tariffs on the nine-nation bloc if it backed alternatives to the dollar, adding to fears that U.S. interest rates could remain higher for longer.

Trump's latest tariff threat was a factor affecting most commodities in trading, leading some analysts to question their assumptions around gold continuing its rise in 2025.

"Gold's post-election slide is somewhat surprising given it was billed as part of the 'Trump trade' in the run up to election," Joe Maher of Capital Economics wrote. "Instead it has faltered following Trump's win while most other Trump Trades have prospered."

While Maher still sees an uptick in gold prices next year, he said the strength of the metal may not be as potent as once believed.

There are growing expectations that the U.S. Federal Reserve will skip a hoped-for interest rate cut in December, although safe-haven demand is keeping the downside for gold prices relatively protected, ANZ Research analysts said.

The market's short-term focus will be fixed on the next Fed meeting, set for mid-December, as well as key U.S. economic data this week including job openings and the non-farm payroll report, ANZ said.

Front-month Comex gold for December delivery (XAUUSD:CUR) finished -0.8% to $2,634.90/oz, snapping a three-session winning streak, and front-month December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) delivery settled -0.7% to $30.449/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Gold could rise to $2,750 by the end of 2025 despite expectations for a stronger dollar and higher Treasury yields, sparked by a "revival" in Chinese demand for gold and central bank purchases, according to Maher.

The "typical negative relationship" between the dollar, real yields and gold prices would seem point to the gold price coming under pressure next year, Maher said, but "the strength of these relationships can vary from year to year."

Maher said he sees "scope for a significant turnaround" next year in China's gold demand, which weakened after strength earlier this year, and "a structurally slowing economy, poor returns from Chinese equities and ongoing problems in the property sector in 2025 are likely to increase the relative attractiveness of gold as an investment among Chinese investors."

data.title: FCPT acquires a NAPA auto parts property for $2M
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT), a real estate investment trust primarily engaged in the ownership and acquisition of restaurant and retail properties, announced on Monday, the acquisition of a NAPA Auto Parts property for $2 million.
  • The property is located in a highly trafficked corridor in New York and is under a corporate, triple net lease with approximately 5 years of term remaining.
  • The transaction was priced at a 7.2% cap rate on rent as of the closing date and exclusive of transaction costs.

data.title: Amazon reveals new data center components to improve efficiency
data.description:
Amazon Web Services (NASDAQ:AMZN) unveiled a new set of data center components to support artificial intelligence and improve efficiencies, as well as several cloud migrations, during the first day of its re:Invent event in Las Vegas today.

"These data center capabilities represent an important step forward with increased energy efficiency and flexible support for emerging workload," said Prasad Kalyanaraman, vice president of Infrastructure Services at AWS. "But what is even more exciting is that they are designed to be modular, so that we are able to retrofit our existing infrastructure for liquid cooling and energy efficiency to power generative AI applications and lower our carbon footprint."

AWS said a more efficient cooling system can reduce mechanical energy consumption by 46% without increasing water usage on a per-megawatt basis.

"By enabling advanced liquid cooling solutions, AI infrastructure can be efficiently cooled while minimizing energy use," said Ian Buck, Nvidia's (NVDA) vice president of hyperscale and HPC.

Migrations to cloud

Amazon also announced Comcast (CMCSA) has moved its 5G wireless core from on premise into AWS. The new cloud-based network is designed to provide Xfinity Mobile and Comcast Business Mobile customers with next-generation 5G wireless services.

Lotus Technology (LOT) has also migrated to AWS to help support its luxury car customers. The automaker plans to transform into a global luxury technology brand by 2028.

Wiwynn, a cloud IT infrastructure provider, announced it has selected AWS Outposts to expand its manufacturing footprint.

"By adopting AWS Outposts, Wiwynn can unlock the full potential of smart manufacturing, driving innovation and production efficiency, and global competitiveness in the Industry 4.0 era," said Wiwynn Chief Information Officer James Wynn.

In addition, Midea, one of the world's largest producers of household and commercial air conditioners, announced it would leverage AWS generative AI to improve its customer relations and to help expand its operations.

data.title: Babcock & Wilcox announces CFO, COO appointment
data.description:
• Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises (NYSE:BW) announced that Lou Salamone will retire as chief financial officer.
  • Cameron Frymyer has been named the next CFO.
  • Chris Riker is named chief operating officer, responsible for B&W’s global business operations.
  • All leadership changes will become effective January 1, 2025.
  • Salamone has entered into an agreement with the company to provide consulting services, including transition support for the chief financial officer role, for a one-year term commencing on January 1, 2025.
  • Frymyer has most recently served as B&W’s senior vice president, business and finance operations.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: M3 announces Kosta Tsoutsis as the new CEO
data.description:
• M3 Metals Corp. (TSXV:MT:CA) announced that Adrian Smith has resigned as president of the company and will now act solely as a director.
  • Kosta Tsoutsis will act as the president and CEO going forward.
  • The company will now have two independent directors, Marco Montecinos and Robert Meister. The three non-independent directors will be Brian Morrison, Adrian Smith and Kosta Tsoutsis.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Art Cashin, prominent NYSE figure, dies at 83
data.description:
Art Cashin, a familiar face to those who work on the New York Stock Exchange floor and those who watch financial news programs on television, has died at 83.

Cashin was well known -- and respected -- by many connected to Wall Street for his astute analysis of the stock market. His decades of experience working at the NYSE combined with his affable personality made him a popular guest on financial news programs. He regularly appeared on CNBC for more than 25 years.

At the time of his death, he was director of floor operations for UBS (NYSE:UBS).

Cashin started at the NYSE in 1964 when he was 23 as a partner at P.R. Herzig & Co. From there, Cashin eventually joined PaineWebber and became part of UBS when the Swiss bank bought the former in 2000.

CNBC's Bob Pisani, reminiscing about his Cashin's life, noted that the man was known for leading the singing of the song “Wait ’Till the Sun Shines, Nellie," an exchange tradition, on Christmas Eve and New Year's Eve.

Pisani also recalled how he was a regular at the NYSE luncheon club, and then at the bar at Bobby Van’s Steakhouse right by the exchange. He added that Cashin always paid in cash and also never used a computer.

Cashin was born in Jersey City, N.J., in 1941. He got his first finance job when he was 17 and in high school. He was hired by brokerage firm Thomson McKinnon.
